title: T-043 — quality scanner pending inspect APIs (06-e)
prompt:
# T-043 — quality scanner pending inspect APIs (06-e)

PRD: docs/prd/06-SCANNER-P1-PRD.md §8.5 FR-SC-BE-053, §8.5 FR-SC-BE-054, §8.5 FR-SC-BE-055
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement scanner-facing Quality pending-inspections, inspect, and failure-reason endpoints.

## Implementation contract
1. Add quality scanner pending-inspections, inspect, and failure-reasons handlers. 2. Enforce org-site RLS and quality.inspect permission. 3. PASS, HOLD, and FAIL update LP qa_status and status per contract; FAIL creates a basic NCR reference. 4. Audit every decision with scanner_audit_log metadata.

## Files
- app/api/quality/scanner/pending-inspections/route.ts [create]
- app/api/quality/scanner/inspect/route.ts [create]
- app/api/quality/scanner/failure-reasons/route.ts [create]
- lib/services/quality/scanner-inspection.ts [create]
- tests/quality/scanner-inspection.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given pending LPs in site context, when GET pending-inspections runs, then only visible org-site LPs with qa_status pending or required WO inspection are returned
2. Given result=pass, when POST inspect runs, then LP qa_status becomes passed and scanner audit is written
3. Given result=fail with reason, when POST inspect runs, then LP qa_status becomes failed, status becomes blocked, and a basic NCR reference is created
4. Given a user without quality.inspect, when any quality scanner endpoint runs, then access is denied server-side

## Test strategy
- RED: write endpoint tests for pending list, pass, fail with NCR, and RBAC denial before implementation
- Run pnpm test quality scanner-inspection

## Risk red lines
- Do not rely on client-side permission guard only
- Do not allow FAIL without failure_reason_id or valid other note
- Do not expose cross-site LPs
